hero.question: Will Ubisoft be acquired before 2027?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point dump in 24 hours signals serious doubt that Ubisoft's activist investors can force a sale, especially with the stock rallying on Star Wars Outlaws hype and management resistance hardening. At 31.5%, you're pricing in maybe one of three realistic buyers (Tencent, Microsoft, Sony) actually moving, but the company's strategic value as a standalone turnaround play is being underpriced against that binary acquisition bet.

daily_take.headline: Ubisoft acquisition odds collapse 10pts, but 31% still looks too high
daily_take.deck: Ubisoft dropped hard overnight on weak earnings and zero M&A chatter from management. The market's pricing in a 31.5% acquisition probability by 2027, which assumes a strategic buyer emerges despite the stock's current funk and Tencent's existing stake complicating any deal. Short this. A struggling Ubi sitting at $30 billion market cap isn't attractive enough to force a buyer's hand when the company's own leadership sounds committed to going solo.
